QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_27

question.text: When an investor voluntarily changes their mutual fund distributor code from one distributor to another, the commission payable is __________.

answer.text: No commission is payable to either the old or new distributor
The old distributor continues to receive trail commission
The new distributor immediately starts receiving trail commission
The trail commission is split equally between the old and new distributor

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When an investor voluntarily changes their distributor code, no commission (including trail commission) is payable to either the old distributor or the new distributor. This rule prevents churning and ensures that distributor changes are not motivated by commission considerations.
**The Trap:** Many assume the new distributor would start earning trail commission since they are now servicing the investor. Others might think the old distributor retains trail on existing AUM. The regulation deliberately makes both parties receive nothing to discourage unnecessary distributor changes.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is a frequently tested rule in NISM V-B. There is one important exception related to distributor cessation of business.
**Key Takeaway:** Voluntary distributor change = zero commission to both old and new distributor. This discourages commission-motivated distributor switching.
